summary: Created cohort-aware drift dashboard under .docs-ops/dashboards/. All validators pass.

changes.description: New: defines page→cohort mapping for eastern (8 pages), western (12 pages via enumerate: expansion), shared (4 pages via enumerate:). Three cohorts: eastern_chat_heavy, western_social_heavy, shared.

New: reads all 4 regex-drift reports + 4 doc-as-test reports, resolves enumerate: entries, groups block counts / issues by cohort, computes health scores (1 - failures/total), writes snapshots/<YYYY-MM-DD>.json. iOS has no by_page field — constructs it by grouping passed/skipped/failures arrays on source_page.

New: reads latest + optional previous snapshot, renders latest-report.md with 5 sections: Headline (metrics + Δ), per-platform drift table, per-platform doc-as-test table, trend table + ASCII sparkline (last 8 snapshots), Alerts.
New: usage docs, schema reference, health score definition, file inventory.
New: placeholder so the snapshots/ directory is tracked by git.
Generated: first snapshot. total_drift=0, total_failures=0, eastern_health=1.0, western_health=1.0.
Generated: first report. Status: all systems healthy.

validators_run: python3 -c "assert all keys present in snapshot schema"
notes: Drift issues and doc-as-test failures are currently 0 across all platforms, so all cohort scores are 1.0 / 100%. The dashboard is ready to track regressions from this baseline. To build trend history, run snapshot.py + render-report.py weekly.
